item.title: EQT Warns of Exit Risks for Alternative Energy Assets Held by PE
item.description: EQT AB, Europe’s biggest private equity firm, says the path to exiting investments in clean-energy developers and operators faces a growing number of hurdles.

item.title: From Cars to Cans: How the Iran War Is Affecting World’s Aluminum Supply
item.description: Aluminum is a ubiquitous metal that’s favored by manufacturers for its abundance and low cost. But the industry is built on a complex and sometimes fragile supply chain.

item.title: Kast’s Campaign to Balance Chile Budget Gets Off to Tough Start
item.description: Chile’s President José Antonio Kast went on TV this week and warned the nation it couldn’t keep racking up debts like it was — then outlined a series of tax cuts and subsidies that may widen the deficit in the near term.

item.title: European Power Prices Set to Close Week Below Pre-War Levels
item.description: European power futures are trading below where they were before the Middle East war began, amid a surge in renewables generation coupled with easing gas prices driven by optimism over a ceasefire.

item.title: US Premarket Movers: Alcoa, Ally Financial, Netflix, NiSource
item.description: S&P 500 Index futures rise 0.3% as of 7:45 a.m. in New York as traders add more risk after the market’s rally.

item.title: Second Mali Gold Mine Scores Backing From Former Olam Exec
item.description: The family office of Indian businessman Gagan Gupta has agreed to fund a second gold mine in Mali, providing a boost to a government that’s been at loggerheads with mining investors in recent years.

item.title: Nike’s Spiraling Stock Has Wall Street Questioning Comeback Plan
item.description: Wall Street is souring on Nike Inc. as concerns over its ability to revamp its business have driven the stock to levels not seen in more than a decade.

item.title: Wartime Supply Crunch Dents LNG’s Appeal to Importers in Asia
item.description: Without guarantees on delivery and price, emerging economies are seeking alternatives to the fuel.

item.title: China Fines Alibaba and PDD for Food Delivery Oversight Failures
item.description: Chinese regulators fined leading food delivery platforms including Alibaba Group Holding Ltd., PDD Holdings Inc. and Meituan for failing to filter out unqualified merchants.

item.title: Goldman Warns Continued Market Recovery Hinges on Rates Relief
item.description: The recent stock market rally that has driven both the S&P 500 and Nasdaq 100 to records needs central banks to pivot back toward rate cuts to sustain its momentum, according to Christian Mueller-Glissmann, head of asset allocation research at Goldman Sachs Group Inc.

item.title: Startup Offers AI Tools to Find New Jobs for Workers Displaced by AI
item.description: Pelgo says it can provide placement services quicker and cheaper using AI

item.title: Iconiq, Go-To Wealth Adviser for Tech’s Elite, Is Putting Billions Into AI
item.description: Last year, Anthropic PBC chief Dario Amodei and a handful of executives traveled 8,000 miles from San Francisco to the Middle East. They were there to meet with some of the world’s most deep-pocketed investors, including Qatar’s sovereign wealth fund and Abu Dhabi-based MGX.

item.title: Anthropic’s Mythos Gatecrashes IMF Meetings
item.description: Financial chiefs in DC can’t stop talking about the latest AI tool.

item.title: Angola on Track for $400 Million Debt-for-Education Swap by June
item.description: Angola plans to conclude a debt-for-education swap of roughly $400 million by June, according to Finance Minister Vera Daves de Sousa.

item.title: Tesla Is Preparing to Launch Six-Seater Model Y Variant in India
item.description: Tesla Inc. is preparing to introduce a new, larger version of its global best-selling electric SUV in India as early as next week, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Nervous Indian Fintechs Push Anthropic for Access to Mythos
item.description: India’s major financial technology firms are pushing Anthropic PBC to give them early access to Mythos, the artificial intelligence model that has sparked global fears about a new era of cyberattacks.

item.title: Japan Sees Defense Budget Just Short of Target at 1.9% of GDP
item.description: Japan’s main defense budget and associated spending for the fiscal year that started in April will total ¥10.6 billion ($66.6 million), equivalent to 1.9% of national gross domestic product, just below Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi’s target of 2%.

item.title: US Cities Where Gas Prices Are Causing the Most Pain
item.description: Prices at the pump have surged everywhere, but it hurts more in cities where driving is the only option.

item.title: Bank of England Expected to Hold Rates This Year, Bloomberg Poll Shows
item.description: Bank of England officials are expected to hold interest rates throughout 2026, a Bloomberg survey of economists showed, despite rising inflation as a result of the energy shock from the Iran war.

item.title: Congo to Top Ethiopia to Become Sub-Saharan Africa’s Fifth-Largest Economy
item.description: The Democratic Republic of Congo is poised to overtake Ethiopia to become sub-Saharan Africa’s fifth-largest economy this year, the International Monetary Fund said, buoyed by a mining boom and a strengthening currency.

item.title: Retail Traders Are Back and Driving All Sorts of Wild Stock Moves
item.description: Photonics, quantum computing are hot as market jumps to new highs.

item.title: UK Energy Stocks Fall as Reeves Eyes Delinking Gas-Power Prices
item.description: UK power stocks dropped after Chancellor of the Exchequer Rachel Reeves said the government was drafting plans to delink gas prices from electricity costs, raising concerns that lower power prices would hurt earnings for generators.

item.title: India Clarifies Gold and Silver Import Confusion Before Festival
item.description: The Indian government has issued a notification on gold and silver imports, after confusion over policy had held up the precious metals at customs, just days before a Hindu festival when buying bullion is considered auspicious.

item.title: Yield Bets Pay Off for Traders Willing to Tune Out War Risks
item.description: Those credit investors who gambled on higher-yielding corporate bonds in the midst of the Iran war are looking increasingly vindicated as markets rebound on hopes of a lasting truce.

item.title: Luxury Watch Revival Cut Short as Iran War Adds to Sector’s Woes
item.description: Before the war in Iran began, the Middle East was one of the few pockets of growth for luxury timepieces.

item.title: OnlyFans Tops $3 Billion Value in Advanced Stake Sale Talks
item.description: OnlyFans, the platform best known for adult content, is in advanced talks to sell a minority stake to Architect Capital in a deal that would value the British company at more than $3 billion.

item.title: South African Unions to Sign 3-Year Eskom Wage Deal for 7% Raise
item.description: South African labor unions will sign a three-year wage deal with state-owned power utility Eskom Holdings SOC Ltd. for above-inflation increases on Friday, according to two of the groups.

item.title: German Carriers Want to Tap Strategic Fuel Reserves, NATO Supply
item.description: German airlines are urging the government to take emergency steps such as releasing strategic kerosene reserves to avoid flight cuts before the peak summer season as Mideast tensions remain high.

item.title: South Africa’s Kganyago Says War Inflation Risks Are Playing Out
item.description: South African Reserve Bank Governor Lesetja Kganyago said the Iran war’s impact on oil prices has validated wariness over inflation, without giving a clear signal on what that means for interest rates.

item.title: HKEX Plans to Cut Trade Settlement to One Day From End of 2027
item.description: Hong Kong’s stock exchange operator is planning to halve the time it takes to settle stock trades, matching a global push toward shorter settlement windows despite concerns over the operational hurdles for Western investors.
